chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, AT KARACHI. Const. Petition No.D-2609 of 2025 Date Order with signature of Judges Fresh Case. 1. For orders on M.A. No.12017/2025. 2. For orders on office objections. 3. For orders on M.A. No.12018/2022. 4. For hearing of bail application. 05.06.2025. Mr. Shahzad Mehmood, Advocate for the Petitioner. ------------------------------------ 1. Urgent application is allowed. 2. Exemption is allowed subject to all just exceptions. 3. The Petitioner has filed his petition through his attorney Hassan Ali Mithani because at present he is in Saudi Arabia for performing HAJJ for protective-cum- transitory bail. The petitioner has been booked in F.I.R. No.08/2025 U/s. 32,32A, 79 and 209 P/U/C, (14-A) & of S. 156 of the Customs Act, 1969 registered at PS Custom House, Karachi. Learned counsel for the petitioner states that the petitioner is completely innocent in this case and that the police are waiting to arrest him in this case on his arrival at Pakistan on account of malafide therefore, he has approached this Court for the grant of protective-cum-transitory bail so that to enable him to approach the concerned trial court and seek pre-arrest bail. Without dilating upon the merits of the case, the Petitioner Usama Mithani son of Muhammad Mithani is granted protective-cum-transitory bail for days from the date of this order so that he may appear before the concerned Court for pre- arrest bail subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.100000/- (Rupees One Lac only) and P.R. Bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Nazir of this Court. This order shall remain operative only for 14 days from today viz. 05.06.2025 and shall cease to exist on 19.06.2025 or when the applicant surrenders before the concerned court, whichever is earlier.

Bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Nazir of this Court. This order shall remain operative only for 14 days from today viz. 05.06.2025 and shall cease to exist on 19.06.2025 or when the applicant surrenders before the concerned court, whichever is earlier. If the petitioner fails to surrender before the concerned trial Court in the aforesaid given period the P.R. bonds shall stands forfeited. The Petition stands disposed of in above terms. HEAD OF CONST. BENCHES. JUDGE
